title: Qnpeps prefixes are hidden while orchid carries provenance
summary: qnpeps:: and the configured Qnpeps identifier prefixes are presentation noise; the remaining qualified name stays orchid so its project provenance is still visible.
details: The hidden family is qnpeps::, Qnpeps on mixed-case types, qnpeps_ on lowercase identifiers, QNPEPS_ on macros, and the shorter qn_ project prefix.
Classification always uses the original token or qualified type before textual stripping.
The rule applies to raw code, declaration overlays, parameter and return types, templates, expressions, and trailing-arrow width calculations.
Strings, comments, includes, static_assert regions, cursor rows, and Visual reveal rows retain source-faithful text under their existing gates.
This settles Qnpeps only; QST-003 remains open for unrelated families such as SDL_.
rationale: The repeated project prefix is high-frequency noise inside Qnpeps code, while orchid is sufficient to retain origin information.
Treating the namespace and identifier prefixes as one family avoids path-dependent spellings.
alternatives: Keep qnpeps:: gray like a generic lowercase namespace; rejected by the owner in favor of hiding it.
Hide the prefix and recolor the remainder as a generic type or identifier; rejected by the classify-before-strip provenance rule.

provenance.note: Owner explicitly completed the earlier fragment with 'qnpeps:: prefix should be hidden'.
Structured stripping/color, raw conceal/color, and width accounting implement the same family.

evidence.claim: Parameter chunks retain DansQnpeps, raw qnpeps:: disappears, Visual round-trips restore, and source bytes stay unchanged.
Actual Neovim RGB output shows hidden prefixes with orchid Config and Boundary names.

questions: Why is Boundary orchid after qnpeps:: disappears?
Which Qnpeps prefix forms are hidden?

last_verified.method: Owner decision implemented across raw, structured, alignment, interaction, and actual RGB capture paths.
